 17→package com.ichi2.preferences
 18→
 19→import android.annotation.SuppressLint
 20→import android.content.Context
 21→import android.content.DialogInterface
 22→import android.util.AttributeSet
 23→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 24→import androidx.preference.ListPreference
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.UIUtils
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.GestureProcessor
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.CardSideSelectionDialog
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.GestureSelectionDialogUtils
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.GestureSelectionDialogUtils.onGestureChanged
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.KeySelectionDialogUtils
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.CardSide
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.*
 37→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.fromGesture
 38→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
 39→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.screenBuilder
 40→import com.ichi2.ui.AxisPicker
 41→import com.ichi2.ui.KeyPicker
 42→import com.ichi2.utils.*
 43→
 44→/**
 45→ * A preference which allows mapping of inputs to actions (example: keys -> commands)
 46→ *
 47→ * This is implemented as a List, the elements allow the user to either add, or
 48→ * remove previously mapped keys
 49→ *
 50→ * Future:
 51→ * * Allow mapping gestures here
 52→ * * Allow maps other than the reviewer
 53→ */
 54→class ControlPreference : ListPreference {
 55→    @Suppress("unused")
 56→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int, defStyleRes: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr, defStyleRes)
 57→
 58→    @Suppress("unused")
 59→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr)
 60→
 61→    @Suppress("unused")
 62→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?) : super(context, attrs)
 63→
 64→    @Suppress("unused")
 65→    constructor(context: Context) : super(context)
 66→
 67→    val screenBuilder: (CardSide) -> Screen
 68→        get() = ViewerCommand.fromPreferenceKey(key).screenBuilder
 69→
 70→    private fun refreshEntries() {
 71→        val entryTitles: MutableList<CharSequence> = ArrayList()
 72→        val entryIndices: MutableList<Int> = ArrayList()
 73→        // negative indices are "add"
 74→        entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_key))
 75→        entryIndices.add(ADD_KEY_INDEX)
 76→        // Add a joystick/motion controller
 77→        entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_axis))
 78→        entryIndices.add(ADD_AXIS_INDEX)
 79→        // Put "Add gesture" option if gestures are enabled
 80→        if (context.sharedPrefs().getBoolean(GestureProcessor.PREF_KEY, false)) {
 81→            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_gesture))
 82→            entryIndices.add(ADD_GESTURE_INDEX)
 83→        }
 84→        // 0 and above are "delete" actions for already mapped preferences
 85→        for ((i, binding) in MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value).withIndex()) {
 86→            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_remove_binding, binding.toDisplayString(context)))
 87→            entryIndices.add(i)
 88→        }
 89→        entries = entryTitles.toTypedArray()
 90→        entryValues = entryIndices.map { it.toString() }.toTypedArray()
 91→    }
 92→
 93→    override fun onClick() {
 94→        refreshEntries()
 95→        super.onClick()
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    /** The summary that appears on the preference */
 99→    override fun getSummary(): CharSequence = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
100→        .joinToString(", ") { it.toDisplayString(context) }
101→
102→    /** Called when an element is selected in the ListView */
103→    @SuppressLint("CheckResult")
104→    override fun callChangeListener(newValue: Any?): Boolean {
105→        when (val index: Int = (newValue as String).toInt()) {
106→            ADD_GESTURE_INDEX -> {
107→                val actionName = title
108→                AlertDialog.Builder(context).show {
109→                    title(text = actionName.toString())
110→
111→                    val gesturePicker = GestureSelectionDialogUtils.getGesturePicker(context)
112→
113→                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) {
114→                        val gesture = gesturePicker.getGesture() ?: return@positiveButton
115→                        val mappableBinding = fromGesture(
116→                            gesture,
117→                            screenBuilder
118→                        )
119→                        if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
120→                            showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_gesture), it)
121→                        } else {
122→                            addBinding(mappableBinding)
123→                            it.dismiss()
124→                        }
125→                    }
126→                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { it.dismiss() }
127→                    customView(view = gesturePicker)
128→
129→                    gesturePicker.onGestureChanged { gesture ->
130→                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(
131→                            fromGesture(
132→                                gesture,
133→                                screenBuilder
134→                            )
135→                        )
136→                    }
137→                }
138→            }
139→            ADD_KEY_INDEX -> {
140→                val actionName = title
141→                AlertDialog.Builder(context).show {
142→                    val keyPicker: KeyPicker = KeyPicker.inflate(context)
143→                    customView(view = keyPicker.rootLayout)
144→                    title(text = actionName.toString())
145→
146→                    // When the user presses a key
147→                    keyPicker.setBindingChangedListener { binding ->
148→                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(
149→                            MappableBinding(
150→                                binding,
151→                                screenBuilder(CardSide.BOTH)
152→                            )
153→                        )
154→                    }
155→
156→                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) {
157→                        val binding = keyPicker.getBinding() ?: return@positiveButton
158→                        // Use CardSide.BOTH as placeholder just to check if binding exists
159→                        CardSideSelectionDialog.displayInstance(context) { side ->
160→                            val mappableBinding = MappableBinding(binding, screenBuilder(side))
161→                            if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
162→                                showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_key), it)
163→                            } else {
164→                                addBinding(mappableBinding)
165→                                it.dismiss()
166→                            }
167→                        }
168→                    }
169→                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { it.dismiss() }
170→
171→                    keyPicker.setKeycodeValidation(KeySelectionDialogUtils.disallowModifierKeyCodes())
172→                }
173→            }
174→            ADD_AXIS_INDEX -> displayAddAxisDialog()
175→            else -> {
176→                val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
177→                bindings.removeAt(index)
178→                value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
179→            }
180→        }
181→        // don't persist the value
182→        return false
183→    }
184→
185→    @SuppressLint("CheckResult") // noAutoDismiss
186→    private fun displayAddAxisDialog() {
187→        val actionName = title
188→        val axisPicker: AxisPicker = AxisPicker.inflate(context)
189→        val dialog = AlertDialog.Builder(context)
190→            .customView(view = axisPicker.rootLayout)
191→            .title(text = actionName.toString())
192→            .negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { it.dismiss() }
193→            .create()
194→
195→        axisPicker.setBindingChangedListener { binding ->
196→            showToastIfBindingIsUsed(MappableBinding(binding, screenBuilder(CardSide.BOTH)))
197→            // Use CardSide.BOTH as placeholder just to check if binding exists
198→            CardSideSelectionDialog.displayInstance(context) { side ->
199→                val mappableBinding = MappableBinding(binding, screenBuilder(side))
200→                if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
201→                    showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_key), dialog)
202→                } else {
203→                    addBinding(mappableBinding)
204→                    dialog.dismiss()
205→                }
206→            }
207→        }
208→
209→        dialog.show()
210→    }
211→
212→    /**
213→     * Return if another command uses
214→     */
215→    private fun bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(binding: MappableBinding): Boolean {
216→        return getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding) != null
217→    }
218→
219→    /** Displays a warning to the user if the provided binding couldn't be used */
220→    private fun showToastIfBindingIsUsed(binding: MappableBinding) {
221→        val bindingCommand = getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)
222→            ?: return
223→
224→        val commandName = context.getString(bindingCommand.resourceId)
225→        val text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName)
226→        UIUtils.showThemedToast(context, text, true)
227→    }
228→
229→    /** @return command where the binding is mapped excluding the current command */
230→    private fun getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding: MappableBinding): ViewerCommand? {
231→        return MappableBinding.allMappings(context.sharedPrefs())
232→            // filter to the commands which have a binding matching this one except this
233→            .firstOrNull { x -> x.second.any { cmdBinding -> cmdBinding == binding } && x.first.preferenceKey != key }?.first
234→    }
235→
236→    private fun addBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
237→        val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
238→        // by removing the binding, we ensure it's now at the start of the list
239→        bindings.remove(binding)
240→        bindings.add(0, binding)
241→        value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
242→    }
243→
244→    /**
245→     * Remove binding from all control preferences other than this one
246→     */
247→    private fun clearBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
248→        for (command in ViewerCommand.entries) {
249→            val commandPreference = preferenceManager.findPreference<ControlPreference>(command.preferenceKey)
250→                ?: continue
251→            val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(commandPreference.value)
252→            if (binding in bindings) {
253→                bindings.remove(binding)
254→                commandPreference.value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
255→            }
256→        }
257→    }
258→
259→    private fun showDialogToReplaceBinding(binding: MappableBinding, title: String, parentDialog: DialogInterface) {
260→        val commandName = context.getString(getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)!!.resourceId)
261→
262→        AlertDialog.Builder(context).show {
263→            title(text = title)
264→            message(text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName))
265→            positiveButton(R.string.dialog_positive_replace) {
266→                clearBinding(binding)
267→                addBinding(binding)
268→                parentDialog.dismiss()
269→            }
270→            negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel)
271→        }
272→    }
273→
274→    companion object {
275→        private const val ADD_AXIS_INDEX = -3
276→        private const val ADD_KEY_INDEX = -2
277→        private const val ADD_GESTURE_INDEX = -1
278→    }
279→}

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.analytics
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.os.Build
 22→import android.webkit.WebSettings
 23→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 24→import androidx.core.content.edit
 25→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalytics
 26→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalyticsConfig
 27→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.httpclient.OkHttpClientImpl
 28→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.request.DefaultRequest
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 33→import com.ichi2.utils.DisplayUtils
 34→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.WebViewDebugging.hasSetDataDirectory
 36→import org.acra.ACRA
 37→import org.acra.util.Installation
 38→import timber.log.Timber
 39→
 40→@KotlinCleanup("see if we can make variables lazy, or properties without the `s` prefix")
 41→object UsageAnalytics {
 42→    const val ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY = "analytics_opt_in"
 43→
 44→    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")
 45→    private var sAnalytics: GoogleAnalytics? = null
 46→    private var sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler: Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler? = null
 47→    private var sOptIn = false
 48→    private var sAnalyticsTrackingId: String? = null
 49→    private var sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = -1
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53→     * Usage after that is static
 54→     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55→     *
 56→     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57→     */
 58→    @Synchronized
 59→    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60→        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61→        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62→            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63→            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64→                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65→                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66→                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67→            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68→                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69→                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70→                .withDefaultRequest(
 71→                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72→                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73→                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74→                        .applicationVersion(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE.toString())
 75→                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76→                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77→                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78→                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79→                )
 80→                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81→                .build()
 82→        }
 83→        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84→        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85→        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86→        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87→            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88→                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89→                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90→                optIn = newValue
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→        return sAnalytics
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97→        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98→            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99→        }
100→        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101→    }
102→
103→    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104→        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105→            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106→        }
107→        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108→    }
109→
110→    fun setDevMode() {
111→        Timber.d("setDevMode() re-configuring for development analytics tagging")
112→        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"
113→        sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = 100
114→        reInitialize()
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119→     */
120→    @Synchronized
121→    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124→            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125→            if (thread == null) {
126→                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127→                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128→            }
129→            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130→        }
131→    }
132→
133→    /**
134→     * Reset the default exception handler
135→     */
136→    @Synchronized
137→    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140→    }
141→
142→    /**
143→     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144→     */
145→    @set:Synchronized
146→    private var optIn: Boolean
147→        get() {
148→            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149→            return sOptIn
150→        }
151→        private set(optIn) {
152→            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153→            sOptIn = optIn
154→            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155→            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156→            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157→            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158→        }
159→
160→    /**
161→     * Set the analytics up to log things, goes to hit validator. Experimental.
162→     *
163→     * @param dryRun set to true if you want to log analytics hit but not dispatch
164→     */
165→    @Synchronized
166→    fun setDryRun(dryRun: Boolean) {
167→        Timber.i("setDryRun(): %s, warning dryRun is experimental", dryRun)
168→    }
169→
170→    /**
171→     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172→     */
173→    @Synchronized
174→    fun reInitialize() {
175→        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176→        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177→        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178→        sAnalytics = null
179→        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180→        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181→    }
182→
183→    /**
184→     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185→     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186→     *
187→     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188→     */
189→    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191→        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192→    }
193→
194→    /**
195→     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196→     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197→     *
198→     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199→     */
200→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202→        if (!optIn) {
203→            return
204→        }
205→        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206→    }
207→
208→    /**
209→     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210→     *
211→     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212→     * @param action   the action the user performed
213→     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214→     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215→     */
216→    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218→        if (!optIn) {
219→            return
220→        }
221→        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222→        if (label != null) {
223→            event.eventLabel(label)
224→        }
225→        if (value != null) {
226→            event.eventValue(value)
227→        }
228→        event.sendAsync()
229→    }
230→
231→    /**
232→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis, parsed from the exception information
233→     *
234→     * @param t     Throwable to send for analysis
235→     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236→     */
237→    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {
238→        sendAnalyticsException(getCause(t).toString(), fatal)
239→    }
240→
241→    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242→    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243→        var cause: Throwable?
244→        var result = t
245→        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246→            result = cause!!
247→        }
248→        return result
249→    }
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253→     *
254→     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255→     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256→     */
257→    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259→        if (!sOptIn) {
260→            return
261→        }
262→        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263→    }
264→
265→    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266→        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267→        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268→
269→        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270→        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271→            true
272→        } else {
273→            hasSetDataDirectory()
274→        }
275→
276→        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277→    }
278→
279→    // A listener on this preference handles the rest
280→    var isEnabled: Boolean
281→        get() {
282→            val userPrefs = AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs()
283→            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284→        }
285→        set(value) {
286→            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287→            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288→                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289→            }
290→        }
291→
292→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
293→    fun resetForTests() {
294→        sAnalytics = null
295→    }
296→
297→    /**
298→     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299→     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300→     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301→     */
302→    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303→        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304→            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305→            try {
306→                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307→                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309→                Timber.w(e)
310→                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311→            }
312→
313→            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314→            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315→            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316→            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317→            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318→            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319→
320→            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321→            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322→            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323→            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324→            try {
325→                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326→                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327→                } else {
328→                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329→                }
330→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331→                Timber.w(e)
332→                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333→                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334→                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335→            }
336→            return this
337→        }
338→    }

24→class ActionButtons {
25→    // DEFECT: This should be private - it breaks the law of demeter, but it'll be a large refactoring to get
26→    // to this point
27→    val status: ActionButtonStatus = ActionButtonStatus()
28→    private var menu: Menu? = null
29→    fun setup(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
30→        status.setup(preferences)
31→    }
32→
33→    /** Sets the order of the Action Buttons in the action bar  */
34→    fun setCustomButtonsStatus(menu: Menu) {
35→        status.setCustomButtons(menu)
36→        this.menu = menu
37→    }
38→
39→    fun findMenuItem(@IdRes resId: Int) = menu?.findItem(resId) as? MenuItemImpl

43→        val RES_FLAG = R.id.action_flag
44→
45→        @IdRes
46→        val RES_MARK = R.id.action_mark_card
47→    }
48→}

26→        get() = R.xml.preferences_controls

29→

36→            .filterIsInstance<ControlPreference>()

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 17→
 18→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 19→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding
 20→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.GestureMapper
 21→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding
 22→
 23→class GestureProcessor(private val processor: ViewerCommand.CommandProcessor?) {
 24→    companion object {
 25→        const val PREF_KEY = "gestures"
 26→    }
 27→    private var gestureDoubleTap: ViewerCommand? = null
 28→    private var gestureLongclick: ViewerCommand? = null
 29→    private var gestureSwipeUp: ViewerCommand? = null
 30→    private var gestureSwipeDown: ViewerCommand? = null
 31→    private var gestureSwipeLeft: ViewerCommand? = null
 32→    private var gestureSwipeRight: ViewerCommand? = null
 33→    private var gestureTapLeft: ViewerCommand? = null
 34→    private var gestureTapRight: ViewerCommand? = null
 35→    private var gestureTapTop: ViewerCommand? = null
 36→    private var gestureTapBottom: ViewerCommand? = null
 37→    private var gestureTapTopLeft: ViewerCommand? = null
 38→    private var gestureTapTopRight: ViewerCommand? = null
 39→    private var gestureTapCenter: ViewerCommand? = null
 40→    private var gestureTapBottomLeft: ViewerCommand? = null
 41→    private var gestureTapBottomRight: ViewerCommand? = null
 42→    private var gestureShake: ViewerCommand? = null
 43→    private val gestureMapper = GestureMapper()
 44→
 45→    /**
 46→     * Whether the class has been enabled.
 47→     * This requires the "gestures" preference is enabled,
 48→     * and [GestureProcessor.init] has been called
 49→     */
 50→    var isEnabled = false
 51→        private set
 52→
 53→    fun init(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
 54→        isEnabled = preferences.getBoolean(PREF_KEY, false)
 55→
 56→        val associatedCommands = HashMap<Gesture, ViewerCommand>()
 57→        for (command in ViewerCommand.entries) {
 58→            for (mappableBinding in MappableBinding.fromPreference(preferences, command)) {
 59→                if (mappableBinding.binding is Binding.GestureInput) {
 60→                    associatedCommands[mappableBinding.binding.gesture] = command
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→        gestureDoubleTap = associatedCommands[Gesture.DOUBLE_TAP]
 65→        gestureLongclick = associatedCommands[Gesture.LONG_TAP]
 66→        gestureSwipeUp = associatedCommands[Gesture.SWIPE_UP]
 67→        gestureSwipeDown = associatedCommands[Gesture.SWIPE_DOWN]
 68→        gestureSwipeLeft = associatedCommands[Gesture.SWIPE_LEFT]
 69→        gestureSwipeRight = associatedCommands[Gesture.SWIPE_RIGHT]
 70→        gestureMapper.init(preferences)
 71→        gestureTapLeft = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_LEFT]
 72→        gestureTapRight = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_RIGHT]
 73→        gestureTapTop = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_TOP]
 74→        gestureTapBottom = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM]
 75→        gestureShake = associatedCommands[Gesture.SHAKE]
 76→
 77→        val useCornerTouch = preferences.getBoolean("gestureCornerTouch", false)
 78→        if (useCornerTouch) {
 79→            gestureTapTopLeft = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_TOP_LEFT]
 80→            gestureTapTopRight = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_TOP_RIGHT]
 81→            gestureTapCenter = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_CENTER]
 82→            gestureTapBottomLeft = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM_LEFT]
 83→            gestureTapBottomRight = associatedCommands[Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM_RIGHT]
 84→        }
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    fun onTap(height: Int, width: Int, posX: Float, posY: Float): Boolean? {
 88→        val gesture = gestureMapper.gesture(height, width, posX, posY) ?: return false
 89→        return execute(gesture)
 90→    }
 91→
 92→    fun onDoubleTap(): Boolean? {
 93→        return execute(Gesture.DOUBLE_TAP)
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    fun onLongTap(): Boolean? {
 97→        return execute(Gesture.LONG_TAP)
 98→    }
 99→
100→    fun onFling(dx: Float, dy: Float, velocityX: Float, velocityY: Float, isSelecting: Boolean, isXScrolling: Boolean, isYScrolling: Boolean): Boolean? {
101→        val gesture = gestureMapper.gesture(dx, dy, velocityX, velocityY, isSelecting, isXScrolling, isYScrolling)
102→        return execute(gesture)
103→    }
104→
105→    fun onShake(): Boolean? {
106→        return execute(Gesture.SHAKE)
107→    }
108→
109→    private fun execute(gesture: Gesture?): Boolean? {
110→        val command = mapGestureToCommand(gesture) ?: return false
111→        return processor?.executeCommand(command, gesture)
112→    }
113→
114→    private fun mapGestureToCommand(gesture: Gesture?): ViewerCommand? {
115→        return when (gesture) {
116→            Gesture.SWIPE_UP -> gestureSwipeUp
117→            Gesture.SWIPE_DOWN -> gestureSwipeDown
118→            Gesture.SWIPE_LEFT -> gestureSwipeLeft
119→            Gesture.SWIPE_RIGHT -> gestureSwipeRight
120→            Gesture.TAP_TOP -> gestureTapTop
121→            Gesture.TAP_TOP_LEFT -> gestureTapTopLeft
122→            Gesture.TAP_TOP_RIGHT -> gestureTapTopRight
123→            Gesture.TAP_LEFT -> gestureTapLeft
124→            Gesture.TAP_CENTER -> gestureTapCenter
125→            Gesture.TAP_RIGHT -> gestureTapRight
126→            Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM -> gestureTapBottom
127→            Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM_LEFT -> gestureTapBottomLeft
128→            Gesture.TAP_BOTTOM_RIGHT -> gestureTapBottomRight
129→            Gesture.DOUBLE_TAP -> gestureDoubleTap
130→            Gesture.LONG_TAP -> gestureLongclick
131→            Gesture.SHAKE -> gestureShake
132→            else -> null
133→        }
134→    }
135→
136→    /**
137→     * Whether one of the provided gestures is bound
138→     * @param gestures the gestures to check
139→     * @return `false` if none of the gestures are bound. `true` otherwise
140→     */
141→    fun isBound(vararg gestures: Gesture): Boolean {
142→        if (!isEnabled) return false
143→
144→        return gestures.any { mapGestureToCommand(it) != null }
145→    }
146→}

109→                Timber.v("waiting for onSoundGroupCompleted")

172→        doAnswer { invocation: InvocationOnMock ->

174→            null
175→        }.whenever(c).setUserFlag(anyInt())
176→        return c

179→    private class CommandTestCardViewer(private var currentCardOverride: Card?) : Reviewer() {

181→            private set

184→            get() = currentCardOverride
185→            set(card) {

187→                currentCardOverride = card
188→            }
189→
190→        override fun performReload() {
191→            // intentionally blank
192→        }
193→
194→        override fun onFlag(card: Card?, flag: Flag) {
195→            lastFlag = flag.code

197→        }
